governing_fact: Every Tesserine surface is a typeset record: the quiet, documentary projection of a machine-readable artifact that remains the product. (design/v1/SYSTEM.md)

members.purpose: the projection as a whole: one reading column on the paper ground
members.occurrence: exactly one — it is the page
members.derivation: Entailment 4 (composed as a record) fixes the parts and their order; entailment 1 (a document's ground) sets the whole on one book-measured column of warm ink on warm paper — a record is one continuous document, not a layout of panels.

members.purpose: opens the surface with the projected record's identity

slots.name: context
slots.content: what kind of record this is and when it was projected

members.derivation: Entailment 4 (composed as a record): a record opens with its identity — which artifact, projected when — closed by the least rule like a nameplate above the fold (entailment 2).

members.occurrence: at most one, immediately after the masthead

slots.name: standfirst
slots.content: the summary the record itself leads with

members.derivation: Entailment 4 (composed as a record): when the record carries a summary, the surface leads with it — the record's words, one step above running matter, at reading weight because a summary is read, not announced.

members.purpose: a titled unit of the record's content
members.occurrence: one or more, mixed freely with section.evidence

slots.name: title
slots.content: the unit's title, from the record

slots.name: heading
slots.content: a heading within the unit, where the record's structure descends

slots.name: metadata
slots.content: the unit's caption-grade matter: dates, authors, references

members.derivation: Entailment 4 (composed as a record): the record's content projects as titled entries in the record's order, separated on the rhythm (entailment 2) rather than boxed.

members.purpose: the record's data shown verbatim: tables, statistics, machine output
members.occurrence: zero or more, mixed freely with section.entry; co-equal content, not decoration

slots.name: caption
slots.content: what the data is and where the record carries it

members.derivation: Entailment 4 (composed as a record): a record's data is co-equal content, so evidence stands as its own form — machine-shaped per entailment 3, framed by the least rule (entailment 2) to mark where verbatim matter begins and ends, never restyled into prose.

members.purpose: closes every surface with provenance and citation of the projected artifact

slots.name: provenance
slots.content: what artifact was projected, at what version, produced how and when

slots.name: citation
slots.content: the citation back to the machine-readable artifact

members.derivation: Entailment 4 (composed as a record) and the fact's own clause that the artifact remains the product: every projection ends by citing its source — a surface without a colophon would present itself as the product, which the fact forbids.
